title: The Death of Lorca
description: The Death of Lorca is a biography by Ian Gibson. Banned briefly in Spain.
descriptionParagraphs: The Death of Lorca is a biography by Ian Gibson. Banned briefly in Spain.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a biography, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Spain. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned briefly in Spain. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: The Death of Lorca entered censorship debates as a biography associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
